message:
fcp-store/durable: cap WAL+snapshot recovery memory to bound DoS surface

`read_wal_records` used `BufRead::read_until(b'\n', &mut raw)`, which
grows `raw` without bound until the delimiter is found. A torn write
(power-loss mid-append of a legitimate large object whose body can be
up to `fcp_cbor::MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES = 64 MiB`, expanding to
~320 MiB in JSON) or any process with WAL write access could cause
recovery to allocate the full line into memory before the parse step
rejected it — OOM-killing the recovery process and blocking startup.

`read_snapshot` had the parallel hazard via `fs::read(path)` with no
metadata check, allowing an oversized or adversarial snapshot file to
be loaded entirely into RAM before parsing.

Fix:

  - Add `MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES = 512 MiB` (sized for worst-case legit
    envelope: 64 MiB body × ~5× JSON overhead + envelope metadata)
  - Add `MAX_SNAPSHOT_BYTES = 4 GiB`
  - Replace `read_until` with `read_until_bounded` — manual `fill_buf`
    /`consume` loop that returns `(bytes_read, hit_cap)` and never
    grows `buf` past `max_bytes`. On cap-hit, recovery treats the
    record as torn (existing truncation path), matching the behavior
    for unparseable records.
  - Pre-check `fs::metadata(path).len()` against `MAX_SNAPSHOT_BYTES`
    in `read_snapshot` before allocating.

Regression test `read_until_bounded_caps_oversized_record_without_oom`
covers four cases: normal newline-terminated record, oversized record
hits cap and returns `hit_cap = true` with `buf.len() <= cap`, empty
input is clean EOF, record exactly at cap is accepted.

All 788 fcp-store tests green, including the existing torn-WAL
recovery tests.

Closes flywheel_connectors-yhmwv.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
